name: Holo Games
summary: Play real retro consoles as streaming κ-addressable content. Five real emulators (Game Boy, NES, GBA, SNES, Genesis) behind one engine-agnostic frontend — drop a ROM, it streams through the κ-ROM seam (verify-on-read) and plays, super-res'd to your display via WebGPU.

description.p: A κ-native retro console player. Five real libretro cores — Gambatte (Game Boy), QuickNES (NES, 6502), mGBA (GBA/GBC/GB, ARM7TDMI), Snes9x (SNES, 65816), and Genesis Plus GX (Genesis, 68000) — built from source with emscripten and driven through ONE engine-agnostic frontend. The execution engine is swappable; the κ substrate is not.

A game is a κ. Every ROM is loaded through the content-addressed κ-ROM seam — streamed by sector, deduped, and re-verified on read before the core sees a byte (Law L5). Drop a .gb / .gbc / .gba / .nes you own and it plays; the κ is its identity, so the same cartridge is shared once per planet.

Each frame is super-resolved on your GPU (Catmull-Rom + sharpening, retina/8K-class) with tile-κ damage so a static screen costs ~0 work; audio runs through a lock-free κ-audio ring into an AudioWorklet. Deterministic cores enable save-states-as-κ, run-ahead (negative latency), and rollback.

ul: Five real systems: Game Boy · NES · GBA · SNES · Genesis (one mGBA core also covers GBC/GB)
Precompiled .holo games auto-import at boot with baked box art + warm-boot streaming (instant time-to-playable)
Drag-drop real ROMs through the content-addressed κ-ROM seam (verify-on-read)
WebGPU super-resolution to your display's physical pixels
Low-latency κ-audio ring → AudioWorklet; keyboard + gamepad
Save states as κ, run-ahead, rollback — on deterministic cores
Serverless: everything runs on your own hardware, no CDN (Law L4)

releases.description: Five real libretro cores (Gambatte / QuickNES / mGBA / Snes9x / Genesis Plus GX), built from source to wasm, driven through one engine-agnostic frontend over the κ substrate. κ-ROM streaming with verify-on-read, precompiled .holo games that auto-import with baked box art + warm-boot streaming, WebGPU super-resolution, lock-free κ-audio, deterministic save-states-as-κ with run-ahead + rollback, and content-addressed games (boot once per planet). Five real CPUs (LR35902 / 6502 / ARM7TDMI / 65816 / 68000).
